name: Torrent Power

gas.notes: SUGEN 1147.5 MW + UNOSUGEN 382.5 MW + DGEN 1200 MW

capacity.source: Torrent Power Investor Presentation 2025, Annual Report FY25

generation.notes: ~10,444 GWh total generation FY25; Gas PLF dropped to 5% in Q4 due to high LNG spot prices; Coal maintained 81% PLF; Distribution 32,263 MU across licensed and franchisee areas
generation.source: Torrent Power Investor Presentation Q4 FY25, PLF calculations

interest_expense.notes: INR 1,045 crore FY25; +10.8% YoY from 943 Cr; Q4 FY25 lowest at 236 Cr due to deleveraging post-QIP

current_assets.notes: INR 8,620.1 crore FY25 (Receivables 6,353 + Cash 289 + Securities 873 + Inventory 726 + Other 379); +1.3% YoY
current_assets.source: Torrent Power Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25

current_liabilities.notes: INR 7,589.5 crore FY25 (Other CL 3,601 + Payables 1,824 + Current LT Debt 1,177 + Accrued 579 + ST Borrowings 250); -2.9% YoY
current_liabilities.source: Torrent Power Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25

operating_cash_flow.notes: INR 4,805 crore FY25; +12.8% YoY from 4,258 Cr; 5-year trend: FY21 2,988 → FY25 4,805 Cr (+60%)
operating_cash_flow.source: Torrent Power Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25

capex.notes: INR ~3,650 crore FY25 (cash flow from investing); Planned: RE INR 57,000 Cr + Thermal INR 60,000-65,000 Cr through 2030
capex.source: Torrent Power Cash Flow Statement FY25

credit_rating.notes: CRISIL AA+/Stable; India Ratings IND AA+/Stable; A1+ short-term

total.notes: -8.71% YoY from 6.77 MtCO2; 56% from gas generation

intensity.notes: Emissions intensity per million USD revenue

net_zero.notes: NO NET-ZERO TARGET ANNOUNCED in official disclosures; one secondary source mentions 2050 aspiration but unconfirmed

tpi_assessment.description: Level 1 - Acknowledging Climate Change as a Business Issue (lowest level)

coal_policy.coal_exit_details: ACTIVELY BUILDING 1,600 MW NEW COAL in Madhya Pradesh

capex_allocation.notes: Thermal capex (60-65k Cr) exceeds RE capex (57k Cr) through 2030 - net thermal expansion

coal_plants.notes: 362 MW operational legacy coal + 1,600 MW NEW COAL under development

plants.name: AMGEN/Sabarmati
plants.notes: Legacy plant, one of India's oldest

under_construction.name: Madhya Pradesh TPP
under_construction.notes: NEW COAL CAPACITY - contrary to global energy transition trends

esg_ratings.notes: TPI Level 1 indicates only basic acknowledgement of climate as business issue; no quantitative GHG targets, no board climate oversight, no climate-linked executive compensation

debt_to_ebitda.notes: Debt 8,840 Cr / EBITDA 5,231 Cr = 1.69x - very strong; improved from 2.25x FY24 post-QIP deleveraging

interest_coverage.notes: EBITDA 5,231 Cr / Interest 1,045 Cr = 5.01x; CRISIL FY24 adjusted 5.18x

ffo_to_debt_pct.notes: OCF 4,805 Cr / Debt 8,840 Cr = 54.4% - exceptional; +17.8pp YoY from 36.6%

current_ratio.notes: CA 8,620 Cr / CL 7,590 Cr = 1.14x; improved from 1.09x FY24; standalone 1.36x

debt_to_capital_pct.notes: Debt 8,840 Cr / (Debt + Equity 27,056 Cr) = 32.7% - very strong; Net D/E 0.40x down from 0.80x post-QIP

credit_metrics_b5.notes: 5/5 metrics PASS. Exceptionally strong credit profile post-₹3,500 Cr QIP equity raise (Feb 2024). FFO/Debt 54.4% and D/EBITDA 1.69x among strongest in India utility sector. Current ratio 1.14x adequate. CRISIL/India Ratings AA+/Stable confirmed Feb 2025. Expected leverage increase to 3.0x FY26 due to ₹22,000-25,000 Cr capex plan.
credit_metrics_b5.source: Calculated from Torrent Power FY25 Consolidated Financial Statements, CRISIL Feb 2025

data_gaps.notes: Scope 3 NOT disclosed per BRSR; company has no SBTi targets or net-zero commitments requiring Scope 3

data_resolved.source: Torrent Power Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25

verification_checklist.coal_capacity_note: 362 MW operational + 1,600 MW NEW COAL under development
verification_checklist.credit_metrics_note: 5/5 B5 metrics calculated: D/EBITDA 1.69x, ICR 5.01x, FFO/Debt 54.4%, CR 1.14x, D/Cap 32.7% - ALL PASS
verification_checklist.net_zero_note: NO net-zero target announced; 2050 aspiration unconfirmed

analyst_notes: Torrent Power is a private, family-controlled Gujarat-focused utility with integrated generation and distribution business. Key characteristics: (1) Gas-dominated generation (46% of FY25 output, 56% capacity) with moderate coal (25% generation, 7.5% capacity); (2) Strong distribution franchise with world-class loss levels (2.81-3.33%); (3) AA+ credit rating with exceptionally strong financials post-₹3,500 Cr QIP. B5 CREDIT ANALYSIS (FY25 complete): D/EBITDA 1.69x (threshold <4.0x) ✓, Interest Coverage 5.01x (threshold >2.5x) ✓, FFO/Debt 54.4% (threshold >20%) ✓, Current Ratio 1.14x (threshold >1.0x) ✓, Debt/Capital 32.7% (threshold <60%) ✓. All 5/5 metrics PASS - among strongest credit profiles in India utility sector. CRITICAL CONCERN: Company is ACTIVELY BUILDING 1,600 MW NEW COAL in Madhya Pradesh (INR 22,000 Cr investment) while simultaneously targeting 10 GW renewables. Thermal capex (INR 60-65k Cr) EXCEEDS RE capex (INR 57k Cr) through 2030. TPI Level 1/5 (lowest) and DitchCarbon 13/100 indicate minimal climate governance. No net-zero target, no SBTi commitment, no board climate oversight. Scope 3 not disclosed. ETCB outcomes: B1 FAIL (100% coal at-risk under methodology), B2 PASS (CCE 6.9% at medium carbon price - gas-dominated), B3 PASS (no LCOE crossover), B4 FAIL (no net-zero, no SBTi, building coal, RE share only 36%), B5 PASS (5/5 metrics). Final score: 3/5 Medium tier. KEY PARADOX: Strongest credit profile in India sample but worst transition governance (TPI Level 1/5). Exemplifies tension between financial stability and climate transition credibility.
